commonProblems.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes-Benz GLS delivers premium refinement and heavy-duty towing capability, but its complex engine families are not immune to high-mileage wear. Replacement demand is heavily driven by the OM642 V6 diesel timing chain system, M276/M278 petrol VANOS solenoid degradation, and AdBlue emission faults in modern straight-six diesels. Every rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to permanently address the original failure point, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether a targeted repair or a full engine replacement offers the best long-term value for your SUV.

problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLS Timing Chain Failure - Why OM642 V6 Diesel Engines Suffer Premature Wear
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLS 350d, GL 350 CDI, GLS 350 BlueTEC (2006-2019, OM642)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner relies on consistent oil pressure and precise tolerances. Over time, chain stretch and plastic guide wear cause slack, leading to cold-start rattling and eventual timing misalignment if maintenance intervals are extended or sub-standard filters are used.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Guide Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain kit swap with upgraded tensioner, guide rails and oil pump chain inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life by 40,000-60,000 miles if caught before severe rattling occurs

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned OM642 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with reinforced timing assembly, new ancillaries and bench-tested calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution with 12-month warranty; eliminates cumulative internal wear

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes-Benz GLS worth £12,000-£25,000, early chain replacement is only cost-effective if the block is otherwise pristine. Once cold-start rattle exceeds 65,000 miles, chain slack has usually damaged tensioners and guides internally. We typically recommend a rebuilt unit, as it resolves timing issues while simultaneously refreshing the oil cooler and turbo seals.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz GLS

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLS Oil Cooler Seal Leak
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLS Oil Cooler Seal Leak - OM642 & OM656 Diesel Failure Pattern
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLS 350d, GLS 400d, GLS 450d (2013-present, OM642 / OM656)
problems.rootCause: Factory-fitted rubber oil cooler gaskets harden under repeated heat cycles from the engine's thermal management system. Degraded seals allow pressurised oil to escape past the V6 valley, contaminating ancillary components and risking low-oil-pressure damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cooler, replace factory seals with reinforced aftermarket units, clean oil pathways
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable for 30,000-40,000 miles; does not address internal engine wear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Gasket Set & Timing Cover Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler seals alongside front timing cover gaskets and valley pan inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive seal refresh; ideal if oil consumption remains otherwise normal

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of upgraded unit with reinforced oil cooler housing and factory-spec gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates leak permanently; ideal if internal scoring or bearing wear is present

problems.recommendation: If the leak is isolated and oil pressure remains stable, a targeted seal replacement is highly cost-effective and should be your first step. However, if you notice persistent oil loss accompanied by blue exhaust smoke or timing chain rattle, the leak is likely a symptom of broader internal degradation. In those cases, a rebuilt diesel engine offers far better long-term security.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM642 / OM656 Mercedes-Benz GLS engine replacement

problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLS VANOS Solenoid Failure - M276 & M278 Petrol V6/V8 Timing Issues
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLS 400, GLS 450 (pre-MHEV), GLS 500 (2013-2019, M276 / M278)
problems.rootCause: The variable valve timing system relies on electromechanical VANOS solenoids to regulate oil flow to cam phasers. Contaminated oil or failing solenoid coils cause erratic phasing, triggering limp mode, rough idle and check engine lights.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - VANOS Solenoid Swap & Oil Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace inlet/exhaust solenoids, perform engine oil flush with manufacturer-spec fluid
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves electrical/coil faults; requires strict oil change intervals to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Camshaft Phaser & Timing Chain Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace solenoids, inspect cam phasers for wear, refresh timing tensioners
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses mechanical binding; recommended if solenoids alone do not clear fault codes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete M276/M278 swap with updated VANOS components, bench-tested and coded
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with warranty; prevents cascading timing damage on high-mileage units

problems.recommendation: For early-stage VANOS faults, solenoid replacement is usually the most sensible route and often resolves limp mode instantly. We advise pairing this with a high-spec oil change. If the solenoids have already failed catastrophically, cam phaser scoring is likely, and timing chain stretch may be present. At that point, investing in a rebuilt petrol engine is the safer financial decision for preserving GLS 400/500 resale value.
problems.cta: Compare M276/M278 Mercedes-Benz GLS petrol engine prices

problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLS AdBlue System Malfunction - OM656 & OM642 BlueTEC Emission Faults
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLS 350d BlueTEC, GLS 400d, GLS 450d (2013-present, OM642 / OM656)
problems.rootCause: The selective catalytic reduction (SCR) system uses a heated AdBlue tank, dosing pump and NOx sensors. Crystallisation of urea fluid or heater element burnout causes blockages, triggering countdown warnings and eventual engine start prevention.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Tank Clean & Heater Element Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain and flush SCR tank, replace heating element, recalibrate dosing pump
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early crystallisation; does not fix damaged NOx sensors

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tank, dosing module, NOx sensors and update ECU software
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive emissions fix; restores compliance and removes start limits

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Emission Hardware
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt diesel engine paired with verified SCR hardware and coding
repairOptions.longevity: Guarantees emissions compliance; eliminates risk of legacy tank design flaws

problems.recommendation: AdBlue faults are primarily emission-system issues, not core mechanical failures. If your GLS still runs smoothly but displays a countdown warning, a specialist SCR overhaul is significantly cheaper and entirely sufficient. However, if the fault has triggered repeated limp modes and high soot accumulation, the DPF and turbo are often compromised. In that scenario, a rebuilt engine with integrated emission components provides a complete reset.
problems.cta: Compare OM656 diesel engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz GLS

problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLS Turbocharger Failure - High-Boost Diesel & AMG V8 Wear
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLS 400d, GLS 350d, GLS 63 AMG, AMG GLS 63 (2013-present, OM656, M177/M157)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo vanes accumulate carbon deposits from EGR recirculation, causing sticking actuators and over-boost conditions. Sustained high-temperature operation accelerates bearing wear, leading to oil burning and catastrophic shaft failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit remanufactured turbo, clean intercooler pipelines, replace actuator gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure; requires flawless oil maintenance to avoid repeat failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Twin Turbo Upgrade & ECU Recalibration
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both units on V6/V8 applications, recalibrate wastegate control mapping
repairOptions.longevity: Optimised for modern driving patterns; extends lifespan by reducing soot recirculation

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with factory-spec turbos, reinforced oil feeds and warranty coverage
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates root cause if internal scoring or bearing damage has already occurred

problems.recommendation: Isolated turbo failure on a well-maintained GLS 400d or AMG 63 is best addressed with a high-quality remanufactured unit and intercooler flush. This preserves the original block and keeps costs predictable. However, if turbo shaft failure has showered metal debris into the intake or oil sump, internal contamination is inevitable. For those cases, a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded oil feed lines is the only reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M177 & OM656 Mercedes-Benz GLS engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes-Benz GLS
finalCta.paragraph: From OM642 timing chain degradation to modern OM656 AdBlue faults, Mercedes-Benz GLS engine failures are rarely simple fixes. Attempting piecemeal repairs on a luxury SUV often leads to recurring faults, inflated dealer invoices, and compromised towing capability. EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK specialists who stock precision-rebuilt GLS engines designed with upgraded components to permanently resolve known weak points. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery and optional mobile supply & fit. Compare live quotes today and secure a specialist-grade replacement without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes-Benz GLS and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes-Benz GLS is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and documented failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1L OM651 four-cylinder diesel served as the entry-level powerplant for the X166 GLS 250d, delivering respectable torque for a large SUV while prioritising fuel economy and lower tax bands. Its compact twin-turbo design made it popular with fleet and private owners seeking reduced running costs without sacrificing the GLS's commanding presence.
commonFailurePoints: balancer shaft bearing wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLS 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L V6 and later straight-six diesel engines form the backbone of the Mercedes-Benz GLS diesel lineup, powering the high-volume GLS 350d and 400d variants across all three generations. The OM642 V6 brought smooth refinement and strong towing capability, while the updated OM656 inline-six introduced sequential turbocharging and improved emissions compliance for the modern X167 platform.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The rare 4.0L OM629 V8 diesel was exclusively fitted to the X164 GL 420 CDI, offering exceptional low-end torque for heavy towing and off-road capability in the earliest generation of Mercedes' full-size luxury SUV. Its complex piezoelectric injection system delivered strong performance but requires specialist knowledge for reliable long-term maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: high-pressure fuel pump wear under heavy load
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L petrol V6 family, spanning the naturally aspirated M272 and twin-turbo M276, provided smooth, refined power for the GLS 350 and 400 variants before the arrival of electrified straight-six units. These engines balanced effortless cruising with reasonable efficiency, though the M272's balance shaft design demands careful attention to oil change intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear on M272
timing chain tensioner rattle on M276
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.5L M272 V6 powered the entry-level GL 350 petrol models in the X164 generation, offering naturally aspirated smoothness and predictable power delivery for owners prioritising refinement over outright performance. Its aluminium construction helped offset the GL's substantial kerb weight, though balance shaft wear remains a known consideration on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Intake manifold runner flap actuator sticking
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L biturbo V8 family, comprising the AMG-tuned M177 and luxury-oriented M176, represents the pinnacle of GLS petrol performance, delivering brutal acceleration and effortless overtaking capability. These hand-built units feature advanced thermal management and cylinder deactivation technology, though sustained high-load driving can accelerate turbo bearing wear without meticulous maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder liner scoring under sustained high load
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.7L M278 twin-turbo V8 powered the GLS 500 across the X166 generation, bridging the gap between everyday usability and serious towing capacity with strong mid-range torque and refined power delivery. Its direct injection system and cylinder-specific knock control improved efficiency over previous V8s, though timing chain tensioner wear can emerge on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 5.5L Petrol Engines
commonFailurePoints: Intake manifold solenoid failure on M273
rod bearing wear on M157 AMG units
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLS 5.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 3.0L M256 straight-six petrol with 48-volt mild-hybrid assist debuted in the X167 GLS 450, combining seamless electric torque fill with improved fuel economy and reduced emissions. Its integrated starter-generator enables smooth stop-start operation and brief electric-only coasting, though the complex electrical architecture demands specialist diagnostic equipment for fault resolution.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes-Benz GLS 3.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 4.0L M176 V8 with mild-hybrid technology powers the GLS 580, offering AMG-derived performance with cylinder deactivation and electric assist for effortless grand touring capability. This sophisticated powertrain delivers exceptional refinement and strong towing capacity, though the intercooler system and turbocharger oil feeds require regular inspection to prevent heat-related degradation. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes-Benz GLS needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes-Benz GLS specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes-Benz GLS 4.0L hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major Mercedes-Benz GLS engine changes across three generations, helping owners quickly identify their exact powerplant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The inaugural X164 GL introduced Mercedes’ first true luxury SUV, pairing rugged body-on-frame capability with refined V6 and V8 powertrains.
keyChanges: Launch of X164 architecture replacing the ML-based SUV concept
Introduction of OM642 3.0L V6 diesel and M272 3.5L petrol
M273 4.6L/5.5L V8 added for flagship towing capability
OM629 4.0L V8 diesel launched for heavy-duty European markets
knownFor: Robust ladder-frame chassis delivering exceptional off-road articulation
M272 balance shaft gear wear on early 2006-2007 production runs
OM642 oil cooler seals prone to degradation by 60,000 miles
Heavy curb weight accelerates brake and suspension component fatigue
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2009 Mercedes-Benz GL replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes-Benz GL 350 CDI engine replacement • 2006 Mercedes-Benz GLS OM642 timing chain issues • Mercedes-Benz GL 420 CDI injector failure
enquiries: 2006 Mercedes-Benz GLS OM642 timing chain issues

years.description: The facelifted X164 GL received updated transmission calibrations, refined engine management, and improved emissions compliance ahead of the next generation.
keyChanges: 7G-Tronic Plus transmission mapping updates for smoother shifts
OM642 BlueTEC integration with AdBlue SCR systems
Exterior and interior facelift improving cabin ergonomics
Pre-transition engineering for X166 unibody architecture
knownFor: Improved OM642 reliability with updated oil cooler gasket materials
Strong residual values for well-maintained LCI examples
M273 intake manifold flap actuator failures remain common
High towing usage accelerates rear suspension bushing wear

years.description: Mercedes shifted to a unibody architecture, significantly improving on-road handling, cabin refinement, and fuel efficiency for the GLS lineage.
keyChanges: Complete platform change to monocoque X166 architecture
OM651 2.1L four-cylinder diesel introduced for entry-level 250d
M276 3.0L twin-turbo V6 replaced naturally aspirated M273
M278 4.7L twin-turbo V8 added with improved thermal management
knownFor: Superior highway stability and reduced NVH compared to X164
OM651 injector copper seal leaks drive early replacement enquiries
M276 timing chain tensioner rattle emerges on cold starts
Air suspension compressor failures common due to moisture ingress
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Mercedes-Benz GL replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes-Benz GL 250d engine replacement • 2013 Mercedes-Benz GL 350d OM642 oil leak repair • Mercedes-Benz GL 400 timing chain cost
enquiries: 2013 Mercedes-Benz GL 350d OM642 oil leak repair

years.preview: X166 GLS Rebrand & OM656 Straight-Six Update
years.description: The mid-life refresh officially renamed the model to GLS and introduced the advanced OM656 3.0L inline-six, marking a major engineering shift.
keyChanges: Global rebrand from GL to Mercedes-Benz GLS (X166 LCI)
OM656 straight-six diesel replaced the V6 OM642 for 400d variant
Updated infotainment and driver assistance suite integration
Refined 9G-Tronic automatic transmission across all models
knownFor: OM656 sequential turbocharging delivers seamless power and torque
GLS 400d quickly became the UK highest-volume replacement target
AdBlue heater and dosing pump faults trigger start-prevention warnings
M278 turbo oil feed line cracking requires proactive inspection
years.ticker: Mercedes-Benz GLS 350d engine replacement • 2017 Mercedes-Benz GLS 400d OM656 AdBlue fault • Mercedes-Benz GLS 500 M278 turbo cost

years.description: The third-generation X167 debuted with a fully electrified mild-hybrid architecture, advanced air suspension, and the hand-built M177 AMG V8.
keyChanges: New X167 platform with 48-volt MHEV across all petrol variants
M256 3.0L straight-six petrol replaces M276 V6 completely
M177 4.0L V8 biturbo introduced for AMG GLS 63
EQ Boost starter-generator integrated into transmission bellhousing
knownFor: Exceptional ride quality with E-ACTIVE BODY CONTROL option
M256 ISG coupling wear documented on early X167 production
M177 turbo bearing sensitivity under sustained high-RPM use
Complex hybrid ECU coding requires specialist diagnostic tools
years.cta: Enquire about 2019-2021 Mercedes-Benz GLS engine availability
years.ticker: Mercedes-Benz GLS 450 MHEV engine replacement • 2020 Mercedes-Benz AMG GLS 63 M177 rebuild cost • Mercedes-Benz GLS 400d OM656 issues
enquiries: 2020 Mercedes-Benz AMG GLS 63 M177 rebuild cost

years.description: The current X167 GLS benefits from software updates, refined turbo calibrations, and enhanced emission controls to meet modern UK compliance standards.
keyChanges: Mid-cycle software and transmission mapping refinements
Strengthened OM656 AdBlue heating elements to prevent crystallisation
M256 and M177 high-pressure fuel pump updates for reliability
Enhanced 4MATIC+ torque distribution algorithms
knownFor: Improved long-term reliability of MHEV starter-generator components
OM656 AdBlue system faults significantly reduced in post-2022 models

faq.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mercedes-Benz GLS engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS engine prices typically range from £3,400 to £13,500 depending on variant, engine code and condition. The OM642 diesel in GLS 350d models sits around £3,400-£5,500, while AMG M177 V8 units command £7,500-£13,500. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: OM642 V6 diesel rebuilt units average £3,400-£5,500 supply-only
M177 AMG V8 petrol engines range £7,500-£13,500 due to complexity
Supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,500 depending on workshop location
Reconditioned units cost more than used but include warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mercedes-Benz GLS?
items.answer: Yes, replacing a Mercedes-Benz GLS engine is often worthwhile given the vehicle's high residual value and towing capability. A rebuilt unit with 12-month warranty typically costs less than purchasing a comparable replacement SUV, preserving your investment.
keyPoints: GLS residual values remain strong, supporting repair over replacement
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing original failure points
Supply & fit services minimise downtime for primary family vehicles
Warranty coverage protects against repeat failure risks
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLS engine replacement and compare value

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz GLS engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The OM656 3.0L straight-six diesel in GLS 400d models offers the best balance of refinement and reliability among Mercedes-Benz GLS engines. Post-2017 units benefit from updated AdBlue systems and improved timing components compared to earlier OM642 V6 designs.
keyPoints: OM656 straight-six diesel features sequential turbocharging and refined internals
Post-2017 GLS 400d models show reduced oil cooler seal failures
M256 mild-hybrid petrol offers smooth operation with electric assist
Regular oil changes with manufacturer-spec fluid extend all engine lifespans
items.cta: Find a reconditioned OM656 Mercedes-Benz GLS engine with warranty

items.question: What causes Mercedes-Benz GLS OM642 engine failure?
items.answer: OM642 engine failures in Mercedes-Benz GLS models most commonly stem from oil cooler seal deterioration and timing chain guide wear. External oil leaks from the V6 valley can lead to low oil pressure if ignored, while chain stretch causes cold-start rattling.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seals harden between 60,000-90,000 miles causing external leaks
Timing chain tensioner wear triggers cold-start rattle on high-mileage units
EGR valve carbon buildup restricts airflow and triggers limp mode
Rebuilt OM642 units include reinforced seals and upgraded tensioners
items.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes-Benz GLS engine replacement from specialists

items.question: Can I swap a GLS 350d engine for a 400d unit?
items.answer: Swapping a Mercedes-Benz GLS 350d OM642 engine for a 400d OM656 unit is technically complex and not recommended without specialist ECU coding and emissions system recalibration. Compatibility issues with wiring harnesses and AdBlue components often outweigh performance benefits.
keyPoints: OM642 and OM656 use different engine management architectures
AdBlue dosing systems and sensor calibrations are not cross-compatible
4MATIC transmission software may require dealer-level reprogramming
Insurance and DVLA documentation must reflect any major powertrain changes
items.cta: Compare compatible Mercedes-Benz GLS engine options for your variant

items.question: Should I choose petrol or diesel for a Mercedes-Benz GLS replacement?
items.answer: Diesel Mercedes-Benz GLS engines like the OM642 and OM656 remain the preferred choice for UK owners prioritising towing capacity and motorway economy. Petrol M256 and M177 units offer smoother refinement but higher fuel costs and lower torque for heavy loads.
keyPoints: Diesel GLS variants deliver 20-30% better fuel economy on long journeys
OM642 and OM656 provide superior low-end torque for towing caravans
Petrol M256 mild-hybrid offers seamless electric assist and reduced NVH
AdBlue system maintenance is required for all modern diesel GLS models
items.cta: Compare diesel and petrol Mercedes-Benz GLS engine prices and availability

items.question: How long should a Mercedes-Benz GLS engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Mercedes-Benz GLS engines typically reach 150,000-200,000 miles before requiring major overhaul or replacement. OM656 straight-six diesels and M256 mild-hybrid petrol units show improved longevity over earlier OM642 and M276 designs when serviced to manufacturer schedules.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with MB 229.51 spec fluid prevent premature wear
Timing chain inspection recommended at 80,000 miles on OM642 units
AdBlue system servicing prevents start-prevention faults on OM656 engines
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can exceed original lifespan expectations
items.cta: Enquire about long-warranty Mercedes-Benz GLS rebuilt engine options

items.question: What are the warning signs my Mercedes-Benz GLS needs an engine replacement?
items.answer: Key indicators that your Mercedes-Benz GLS requires engine replacement include persistent cold-start rattling, blue exhaust smoke, low oil pressure warnings, and repeated limp mode activation. These symptoms often signal internal wear beyond economical repair on OM642 and M276 engines.
keyPoints: Cold-start metallic rattling suggests OM642 timing chain tensioner failure
Blue smoke indicates piston ring or turbo seal degradation requiring rebuild
Low oil pressure warnings often precede catastrophic bearing failure
Repeated AdBlue faults on OM656 may mask deeper combustion issues
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLS engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: Do Mercedes-Benz GLS replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLS engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal mechanical components. Specialist rebuilders often offer extended 24-month coverage on premium units like OM656 and M177 engines.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty is standard across our specialist network
Coverage includes internal components, gaskets and seals post-installation
Extended 24-month options available for OM656 and AMG M177 units
Warranty terms vary by supplier; always confirm exact coverage before purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Mercedes-Benz GLS replacement engines from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I find the engine code on my Mercedes-Benz GLS?
items.answer: The engine code on your Mercedes-Benz GLS appears on the engine block stamping, vehicle registration document (V5C), and under the bonnet on the emissions compliance plate. OM642, OM656, M256 and M177 are the most common codes across X164, X166 and X167 generations.
keyPoints: V5C registration document lists engine code in section D.2
Emissions plate under bonnet displays code format like "OM642.826"
Engine block stamping visible near oil filter housing on most variants
Registration lookup instantly identifies exact engine code and compatible replacements
items.cta: Enter your registration to identify your Mercedes-Benz GLS engine code and get quotes
